commands.review_summary:
The PR preserves `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms`, changes its implicit iMessage debounce from 2500 ms to 7000 ms, narrows URL-balloon coalescing to adjacent pairs, and updates iMessage docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +218, Docs -2. Total +225 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source/log-level: current main uses a 2500 ms opt-in window, while the PR proof reports a real Messages.app URL-preview row arriving about 6 seconds after the command row. I did not run a live local Messages.app reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The protected maintainer label, changed opt-in default, message-delivery tradeoff, and linked teardown issue require human merge/product judgment rather than an automation repair lane.

target.title: fix(imessage): keep split-send coalescing opt-in
target.body:
## Summary

Related to #91243. This PR keeps the compatibility path and narrows its behavior; #91243 should remain open for the eventual teardown decision unless maintainers explicitly decide this is the final resolution.

This keeps the opt-in `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms` compatibility path instead of deleting it. The branch now addresses the review risk by preserving the fallback for command-only URL split-sends while tightening the metadata-aware behavior when `imsg` exposes `balloon_bundle_id`.

## What changed

- Preserved `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms` in the config/type surface.
- Keeps iMessage DM split-send debouncing only when the opt-in flag is enabled and no explicit global or iMessage inbound debounce is configured.
- Widens that opt-in default from 2500 ms to 7000 ms after live Messages.app proof showed URL preview rows can arrive ~5-6 seconds after the text row.
- Uses `balloon_bundle_id: "com.apple.messages.URLBalloonProvider"` to merge only the adjacent command + URL preview pair when metadata is available.
- Keeps ordinary buffered DMs separate once any balloon metadata has been observed, including metadata observed on stale/suppressed rows.
- Keeps the metadata-less fallback for older or pre-latch `imsg` sessions, where a command + URL split-send cannot be distinguished from ordinary same-sender rows.
- Updates iMessage and BlueBubbles migration docs to describe the preserved opt-in behavior and fallback limits.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: iMessage command + URL preview split-sends should still reach the agent as one turn without regressing ordinary DM separation when structural `imsg` balloon metadata is available.

Real environment tested: Local macOS Messages.app + `imsg 0.11.1` connected to Messages.app with `privateApi.available: true` and `v2Ready: true`; foreground OpenClaw gateway from this PR checkout at `638b3851ddd`; plus the iMessage monitor through mocked `imsg` RPC notifications and the real monitor/debounce/coalesce code path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm openclaw --version
imsg status --json
pnpm openclaw gateway stop
pnpm openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --verbose
pnpm openclaw channels status --probe --channel imessage --json
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/parse-notification.test.ts
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check
pnpm build
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix:

- Live probe before the fix exposed the original gap: a single user-sent iMessage bubble with `/help ocproof-1782075768` and `https://example.com/ocproof-1782075768` wrote the text row at local 14:05:59 and the URL preview row at 14:06:04, so the old 2500 ms window dispatched them separately as message IDs 782 and 784.
- Live probe after the fix used one user-sent iMessage bubble with `/help ocproof-1782081763` and `https://example.com/ocproof-1782081763`. Messages.app wrote row 6223 at local 15:44:32 with text `/help ocproof-1782081763`, then row 6224 at local 15:44:38 with text `https://example.com/ocproof-1782081763` and `balloon_bundle_id='com.apple.messages.URLBalloonProvider'`.
- The PR-head foreground gateway emitted one merged inbound turn at local 15:44:46: preview contained `/help ocproof-1782081763 https://example.com/ocproof-1782081763`, and the dispatch path used a single `messageId=787` through `message received`, `message queued`, `message processed`, and `outcome=completed`.
- Separate ordinary DMs from the same sender stayed separate in the live run: `ordinary one ocproof-1782075425` and `ordinary two ocproof-1782075425` dispatched as separate message IDs 776 and 778.
- `3 passed (3)` test files, `49 passed (49)` tests in the iMessage monitor/parser shard.
- Config doc baseline check: `OK docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.
- Bundled channel config metadata check exited cleanly.
- `pnpm build` completed successfully.
- Autoreview exited clean: `no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: A real Messages.app command+URL split-send with a 6 second text-to-URL-preview gap merged into one iMessage inbound turn on PR head; URL split-send buckets also merge in tests when a URL balloon row is present; unrelated buffered rows dispatch separately once metadata support is known; stale balloon rows are suppressed from delivery but still latch metadata support for later live rows; old metadata-less sessions preserve the existing fallback merge behavior.

What was not tested: CI is still completing on GitHub for the latest head; local focused tests/build and live iMessage proof passed.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/parse-notification.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
